docs.title: Short example

docs.text: API Reference ¶ Pipeline ¶ sus_pipeline(...) -> runs import, clean, standardize, filter, variables, aggregate. Returns: duckdb.DuckDBPyRelation (lazy). Core ¶ sus_import(system, uf, year, month=None, ..., backend="auto") sus_clean(rel, fix_enc=True, dedup=True, dedup_cols=None, age_range=(0, 120)) sus_standardize(rel, lang="en", system=None) sus_filter(rel, groups=None, codes=None, age_min=None, age_max=None, sex=None, race=None, uf=None, municipality=None, date_start=None, date_end=None) sus_variables(rel, age_group=None, epi_week=False, season=False, quarter=False, month_name=False, day_of_week=False) sus_aggregate(rel, time="month", geo="state", extra_groups=None) I/O and cache ¶ sus_export(data, path, fmt=None, overwrite=True, compress="snappy") sus_cache_info(cache_dir="dados/cache") sus_cache_clear(cache_dir="dados/cache", system=None, uf=None, before=None) collect_arrow(rel) Enrichment ¶ sus_climate(data, climate, time_window=0, lags=None) sus_spatial(data, shapefile=None, geo_level="municipality", join_type="left") sus_census(data, census) sus_fill_gaps(data, method="linear", group_col="municipality_code", date_col="date", max_gap=None) Utilities ¶ sus_explore(what="systems") sus_quality(data) update_climasus_data(...) Important notes ¶ groups= in sus_filter requires local climasus-data . lang="en" and lang="es" in sus_standardize also require local dictionaries. sus_census(..., census=None) is not auto-loaded yet in Python.
docs.title: API Reference

docs.text: sus_pipeline(...) -> runs import, clean, standardize, filter, variables, aggregate. Returns: duckdb.DuckDBPyRelation (lazy).
docs.title: Pipeline

docs.text: sus_import(system, uf, year, month=None, ..., backend="auto") sus_clean(rel, fix_enc=True, dedup=True, dedup_cols=None, age_range=(0, 120)) sus_standardize(rel, lang="en", system=None) sus_filter(rel, groups=None, codes=None, age_min=None, age_max=None, sex=None, race=None, uf=None, municipality=None, date_start=None, date_end=None) sus_variables(rel, age_group=None, epi_week=False, season=False, quarter=False, month_name=False, day_of_week=False) sus_aggregate(rel, time="month", geo="state", extra_groups=None)
docs.title: Core

docs.text: sus_export(data, path, fmt=None, overwrite=True, compress="snappy") sus_cache_info(cache_dir="dados/cache") sus_cache_clear(cache_dir="dados/cache", system=None, uf=None, before=None) collect_arrow(rel)
docs.title: I/O and cache

docs.text: sus_climate(data, climate, time_window=0, lags=None) sus_spatial(data, shapefile=None, geo_level="municipality", join_type="left") sus_census(data, census) sus_fill_gaps(data, method="linear", group_col="municipality_code", date_col="date", max_gap=None)

docs.title: Important notes

docs.text: Architecture ¶ Overview ¶ climasus4py uses DuckDB relations to keep transformations lazy until materialization. Main flow: Import and parquet cache Cleaning and standardization Epidemiological filtering Derived variables Time/geo aggregation Export Modules ¶ src/climasus/core : pipeline and core transforms src/climasus/io : export and cache helpers src/climasus/enrichment : climate, census, spatial, gap fill src/climasus/utils : CID, metadata, quality, exploration Fast path in pipeline ¶ sus_pipeline tries a single optimized SQL query when: age_group is None epi_week is False time is one of year|quarter|month|week|day geo is state or municipality If constraints are not met, it falls back to staged execution. Local cache ¶ Default: dados/cache/<SYSTEM>/<UF>_<YEAR>_<MONTH|all>.parquet Reused automatically with cache=True Shared metadata ¶ Loaded from external climasus-data : CID groups column dictionaries UF and region metadata Without it, parts of the API are unavailable.
docs.title: Architecture

docs.text: Getting Started ¶ 1. Install ¶ pip install climasus4py Optional extras: pip install climasus4py[datasus] pip install climasus4py[spatial] pip install climasus4py[ml] pip install climasus4py[excel] 2. Set up climasus-data ¶ Some API paths require local metadata (CID groups, column dictionaries, UF/region mappings). from climasus import update_climasus_data update_climasus_data() Or clone manually: git clone https://github.com/ClimaHealth/climasus-data.git You can also set CLIMASUS_DATA_DIR . 3. Full pipeline ¶ import climasus as cs rel = cs.sus_pipeline( system="SIM-DO", uf="SP", year=[2021, 2022, 2023], lang="en", groups=["dengue"], time="month", geo="state", ) print(rel.columns) pdf = rel.df() 4. Stage-by-stage usage ¶ import climasus as cs rel = cs.sus_import("SIM-DO", "SP", [2022, 2023]) rel = cs.sus_clean(rel) rel = cs.sus_standardize(rel, lang="en", system="SIM-DO") rel = cs.sus_filter(rel, codes=["A90", "A91"], age_min=0, age_max=80) rel = cs.sus_variables(rel, age_group="who", epi_week=True) rel = cs.sus_aggregate(rel, time="month", geo="state") cs.sus_export(rel, "output/dengue_sp.parquet")
docs.title: Getting Started

docs.text: pip install climasus4py Optional extras: pip install climasus4py[datasus] pip install climasus4py[spatial] pip install climasus4py[ml] pip install climasus4py[excel]

docs.text: Referencia API ¶ Pipeline ¶ sus_pipeline(...) -> ejecuta import, clean, standardize, filter, variables y aggregate. Retorno: duckdb.DuckDBPyRelation (lazy). Core ¶ sus_import(system, uf, year, month=None, ..., backend="auto") sus_clean(rel, fix_enc=True, dedup=True, dedup_cols=None, age_range=(0, 120)) sus_standardize(rel, lang="en", system=None) sus_filter(rel, groups=None, codes=None, age_min=None, age_max=None, sex=None, race=None, uf=None, municipality=None, date_start=None, date_end=None) sus_variables(rel, age_group=None, epi_week=False, season=False, quarter=False, month_name=False, day_of_week=False) sus_aggregate(rel, time="month", geo="state", extra_groups=None) I/O y cache ¶ sus_export(data, path, fmt=None, overwrite=True, compress="snappy") sus_cache_info(cache_dir="dados/cache") sus_cache_clear(cache_dir="dados/cache", system=None, uf=None, before=None) collect_arrow(rel) Enriquecimiento ¶ sus_climate(data, climate, time_window=0, lags=None) sus_spatial(data, shapefile=None, geo_level="municipality", join_type="left") sus_census(data, census) sus_fill_gaps(data, method="linear", group_col="municipality_code", date_col="date", max_gap=None) Utilidades ¶ sus_explore(what="systems") sus_quality(data) update_climasus_data(...) Notas importantes ¶ groups= en sus_filter requiere climasus-data local. lang="en" y lang="es" en sus_standardize tambien requieren diccionarios locales. sus_census(..., census=None) todavia no tiene autoload en Python.

docs.text: Arquitectura ¶ Resumen ¶ climasus4py usa relaciones DuckDB para mantener transformaciones lazy hasta materializar. Flujo principal: Importacion y cache parquet Limpieza y estandarizacion Filtros epidemiologicos Variables derivadas Agregacion temporal/geografica Exportacion Modulos ¶ src/climasus/core : pipeline y transformaciones centrales src/climasus/io : exportacion y cache src/climasus/enrichment : clima, censo, espacial, gap fill src/climasus/utils : CID, metadatos, calidad y exploracion Fast path del pipeline ¶ sus_pipeline intenta una consulta SQL optimizada cuando: age_group is None epi_week is False time en year|quarter|month|week|day geo en state|municipality Si no cumple, usa el flujo por etapas. Cache local ¶ Ruta por defecto: dados/cache/<SYSTEM>/<UF>_<YEAR>_<MONTH|all>.parquet Reutilizacion automatica con cache=True Metadatos compartidos ¶ Se cargan desde climasus-data externo: grupos CID diccionarios de columnas UF y regiones Sin ese repositorio local, partes de la API no funcionan.
docs.title: Arquitectura

docs.text: climasus4py usa relaciones DuckDB para mantener transformaciones lazy hasta materializar. Flujo principal: Importacion y cache parquet Limpieza y estandarizacion Filtros epidemiologicos Variables derivadas Agregacion temporal/geografica Exportacion

docs.text: Primeros Pasos ¶ 1. Instalacion ¶ pip install climasus4py Extras opcionales: pip install climasus4py[datasus] pip install climasus4py[spatial] pip install climasus4py[ml] pip install climasus4py[excel] 2. Configurar climasus-data ¶ Algunas partes de la API usan metadatos locales (grupos CID, diccionarios de columnas, UF/regiones). from climasus import update_climasus_data update_climasus_data() O manualmente: git clone https://github.com/ClimaHealth/climasus-data.git Tambien puedes definir CLIMASUS_DATA_DIR . 3. Pipeline completo ¶ import climasus as cs rel = cs.sus_pipeline( system="SIM-DO", uf="SP", year=[2021, 2022, 2023], lang="en", groups=["dengue"], time="month", geo="state", ) print(rel.columns) pdf = rel.df() 4. Flujo por etapas ¶ import climasus as cs rel = cs.sus_import("SIM-DO", "SP", [2022, 2023]) rel = cs.sus_clean(rel) rel = cs.sus_standardize(rel, lang="en", system="SIM-DO") rel = cs.sus_filter(rel, codes=["A90", "A91"], age_min=0, age_max=80) rel = cs.sus_variables(rel, age_group="who", epi_week=True) rel = cs.sus_aggregate(rel, time="month", geo="state") cs.sus_export(rel, "output/dengue_sp.parquet")
docs.title: Primeros Pasos

docs.text: API de Referencia ¶ Pipeline ¶ sus_pipeline(...) -> executa import, clean, standardize, filter, variables e aggregate. Retorno: duckdb.DuckDBPyRelation (lazy). Core ¶ sus_import(system, uf, year, month=None, ..., backend="auto") sus_clean(rel, fix_enc=True, dedup=True, dedup_cols=None, age_range=(0, 120)) sus_standardize(rel, lang="en", system=None) sus_filter(rel, groups=None, codes=None, age_min=None, age_max=None, sex=None, race=None, uf=None, municipality=None, date_start=None, date_end=None) sus_variables(rel, age_group=None, epi_week=False, season=False, quarter=False, month_name=False, day_of_week=False) sus_aggregate(rel, time="month", geo="state", extra_groups=None) I/O e cache ¶ sus_export(data, path, fmt=None, overwrite=True, compress="snappy") sus_cache_info(cache_dir="dados/cache") sus_cache_clear(cache_dir="dados/cache", system=None, uf=None, before=None) collect_arrow(rel) Enriquecimento ¶ sus_climate(data, climate, time_window=0, lags=None) sus_spatial(data, shapefile=None, geo_level="municipality", join_type="left") sus_census(data, census) sus_fill_gaps(data, method="linear", group_col="municipality_code", date_col="date", max_gap=None) Utilitarios ¶ sus_explore(what="systems") sus_quality(data) update_climasus_data(...) Observacoes importantes ¶ groups= em sus_filter depende de climasus-data local. lang="en" ou lang="es" em sus_standardize tambem depende de dicionarios locais. sus_census(..., census=None) ainda nao faz autoload no Python.

docs.text: Arquitetura ¶ Visao geral ¶ climasus4py usa DuckDB para manter o pipeline lazy ate a materializacao final. Fluxo principal: Importacao e cache em parquet Limpeza e padronizacao Filtros epidemiologicos Variaveis derivadas Agregacao temporal/geografica Exportacao Modulos ¶ src/climasus/core : pipeline e transformacoes centrais src/climasus/io : exportacao e cache src/climasus/enrichment : clima, censo, espacial, gap fill src/climasus/utils : CID, metadados, qualidade e exploracao Fast path do pipeline ¶ su_pipeline tenta um caminho otimizado (SQL unico) quando: age_group is None epi_week is False time em year|quarter|month|week|day geo em state|municipality Se nao atender, usa o pipeline por etapas. Cache local ¶ Diretorio padrao: dados/cache/<SYSTEM>/<UF>_<YEAR>_<MONTH|all>.parquet Reutilizacao automatica quando cache=True Metadados compartilhados ¶ Vem de climasus-data (fora do pacote): grupos CID dicionarios de colunas codigos UF e regioes Sem esse repositorio local, partes da API nao funcionam.
docs.title: Arquitetura

docs.text: climasus4py usa DuckDB para manter o pipeline lazy ate a materializacao final. Fluxo principal: Importacao e cache em parquet Limpeza e padronizacao Filtros epidemiologicos Variaveis derivadas Agregacao temporal/geografica Exportacao
docs.title: Visao geral

docs.text: src/climasus/core : pipeline e transformacoes centrais src/climasus/io : exportacao e cache src/climasus/enrichment : clima, censo, espacial, gap fill src/climasus/utils : CID, metadados, qualidade e exploracao
docs.title: Modulos

docs.text: su_pipeline tenta um caminho otimizado (SQL unico) quando: age_group is None epi_week is False time em year|quarter|month|week|day geo em state|municipality Se nao atender, usa o pipeline por etapas.
docs.title: Fast path do pipeline

docs.text: Diretorio padrao: dados/cache/<SYSTEM>/<UF>_<YEAR>_<MONTH|all>.parquet Reutilizacao automatica quando cache=True
docs.title: Cache local

docs.text: Vem de climasus-data (fora do pacote): grupos CID dicionarios de colunas codigos UF e regioes Sem esse repositorio local, partes da API nao funcionam.
docs.title: Metadados compartilhados

docs.text: Desenvolvimento ¶ Setup local ¶ pip install -e . pip install pytest ruff mkdocs Testes ¶ pytest -q Benchmark ¶ python benchmark/benchmark_pipeline.py Relatorio atual: benchmark/BENCHMARK_REPORT.md . Documentacao MkDocs ¶ mkdocs serve mkdocs build Convencoes recomendadas ¶ Manter funcoes publicas exportadas em src/climasus/__init__.py Priorizar operacoes lazy com DuckDB Evitar materializacao em pandas antes do necessario Atualizar docs trilingues quando houver mudanca de API

docs.title: Convencoes recomendadas

docs.text: Primeiros Passos ¶ 1. Instalacao ¶ pip install climasus4py Extras opcionais: pip install climasus4py[datasus] pip install climasus4py[spatial] pip install climasus4py[ml] pip install climasus4py[excel] 2. Configurar climasus-data ¶ Parte da API depende de metadados locais (grupos CID, dicionarios de colunas, UFs, regioes). from climasus import update_climasus_data update_climasus_data() Ou manualmente: git clone https://github.com/ClimaHealth/climasus-data.git Opcionalmente defina CLIMASUS_DATA_DIR . 3. Pipeline completo ¶ import climasus as cs rel = cs.sus_pipeline( system="SIM-DO", uf="SP", year=[2021, 2022, 2023], lang="en", groups=["dengue"], time="month", geo="state", ) # DuckDB relation lazy print(rel.columns) # Materializar quando precisar pdf = rel.df() 4. Fluxo etapa por etapa ¶ import climasus as cs rel = cs.sus_import("SIM-DO", "SP", [2022, 2023]) rel = cs.sus_clean(rel) rel = cs.sus_standardize(rel, lang="en", system="SIM-DO") rel = cs.sus_filter(rel, codes=["A90", "A91"], age_min=0, age_max=80) rel = cs.sus_variables(rel, age_group="who", epi_week=True) rel = cs.sus_aggregate(rel, time="month", geo="state") cs.sus_export(rel, "output/dengue_sp.parquet")
docs.title: Primeiros Passos

docs.text: pip install climasus4py Extras opcionais: pip install climasus4py[datasus] pip install climasus4py[spatial] pip install climasus4py[ml] pip install climasus4py[excel]
docs.title: 1. Instalacao

docs.text: Parte da API depende de metadados locais (grupos CID, dicionarios de colunas, UFs, regioes). from climasus import update_climasus_data update_climasus_data() Ou manualmente: git clone https://github.com/ClimaHealth/climasus-data.git Opcionalmente defina CLIMASUS_DATA_DIR .
docs.title: 2. Configurar climasus-data

docs.text: import climasus as cs rel = cs.sus_pipeline( system="SIM-DO", uf="SP", year=[2021, 2022, 2023], lang="en", groups=["dengue"], time="month", geo="state", ) # DuckDB relation lazy print(rel.columns) # Materializar quando precisar pdf = rel.df()
docs.title: 3. Pipeline completo

docs.text: import climasus as cs rel = cs.sus_import("SIM-DO", "SP", [2022, 2023]) rel = cs.sus_clean(rel) rel = cs.sus_standardize(rel, lang="en", system="SIM-DO") rel = cs.sus_filter(rel, codes=["A90", "A91"], age_min=0, age_max=80) rel = cs.sus_variables(rel, age_group="who", epi_week=True) rel = cs.sus_aggregate(rel, time="month", geo="state") cs.sus_export(rel, "output/dengue_sp.parquet")
docs.title: 4. Fluxo etapa por etapa
